formdata.value: Initial meeting to disccuss problems of child
Initial meeting to disccuss problems of child

request.description: Lists paginated conversations for the authenticated user (all conversations if the user has the program_staff role).

Starts a new conversation with `user_id` (or reuses the existing 1:1 conversation) and sends the first message. Broadcasts `message.sent` on the `conversation.{id}` private channel. Set the `target_user_id` collection variable to another user's id before running.
Returns conversation details, participants, and all messages.
Sends a message in the conversation and broadcasts `message.sent` on the `conversation.{id}` private Pusher channel in real time.
Adds a user to the conversation. family_member accounts are forbidden from adding participants.

urlencoded.description: Socket ID from the Pusher JS/mobile SDK connection

request.description: Mobile clients call this (with their Passport bearer token) to authorize subscribing to a `private-conversation.{id}` Pusher channel. `socket_id` comes from the connected Pusher client SDK; `channel_name` must be `private-conversation.{conversation_id}`.
DSP-only community feed, most recent first. Requires the `dsp` role (403 otherwise). Each post includes reaction counts, `reacted_by_me`, `is_own`, and `author` (null when `is_anonymous` is true).

formdata.value: Marcus said my name unprompted today. Three months in. I almost cried. This is why we do it.

formdata.description: 1 to hide your identity from other DSPs

request.description: Shares a new post to the feed and broadcasts `post.created` on the `voice-corner` private Pusher channel.

item.name: React to Post (as DSP 1 — collection token)

formdata.description: heart|fire|clap — call again with the same type to remove your reaction

request.description:
Toggles a reaction on the post for whichever DSP is authenticated by the collection's bearer token. Broadcasts `reaction.updated` (post_id + updated counts) on the `voice-corner` private channel.

Note: calling this twice in a row with the SAME token toggles the reaction off again (like/unlike) — it will NOT increment the count a second time. To see the count go up, react as a DIFFERENT DSP account (see "Login as DSP 2" + "React to Post (as DSP 2)" below).

formdata.description: Socket ID from the connected Pusher JS/mobile SDK

request.description: Mobile clients call this (with their Passport bearer token) to authorize subscribing to the shared `private-voice-corner` Pusher channel. Only users with the `dsp` role are authorized.

query.description: Optional: filter by family admin's user id

request.description: Returns the authenticated DSP's own daily logs, paginated.

formdata.description: Required: user id of a family_admin assigned to this DSP (i.e. sharing a conversation with them) — see GET /dsp/clients for the list

formdata.value: Had a great day, participated in all morning activities without prompting.
Arts and crafts, short walk outside

formdata.description: Laravel method spoofing — required because multipart/form-data bodies aren't parsed on native PUT requests

formdata.value: Updated note: afternoon mood improved after lunch.

request.description: All fields are optional on update — only send what you want to change. Uses POST + _method=PUT spoofing so Postman's multipart form-data body is parsed correctly by Laravel.

Profile screen for the authenticated DSP: identity (name, role, profile_photo, status), this_week (shifts_logged = count of appointment-type calendar events this week, hours = their total duration), certifications, and assigned_clients (same list as GET /dsp/clients).
Lists the authenticated DSP's certifications. Each entry includes `status` (current|expiring_soon|expired) and `status_label` (e.g. "Current" or "Expires Jun 2026").

formdata.description: Optional: leave blank for certifications that don't expire
Laravel method spoofing — required because multipart/form-data bodies aren't parsed on native PUT requests
